name: Nazi Antarctica
description: The concept of "Nazi Antarctica" encompasses various conspiracy theories alleging that Nazi Germany established a secret base or colony in Antarctica, either during World War II or in its aftermath. These theories often involve advanced technology, UFOs, and connections to esoteric beliefs. Proponents claim that Operation Highjump, a large-scale U.S. Navy expedition to Antarctica in 1946-1947, was actually a military operation intended to locate and destroy this Nazi base, known as "Neuschwabenland" (New Swabia), a region of Antarctica explored by a German expedition in 1938-1939. Some theories suggest the base still exists, harboring surviving Nazis and advanced technology. Evidence cited includes alleged inconsistencies in historical accounts, unexplained aerial phenomena reported during Operation Highjump, and interpretations of Nazi interest in esoteric subjects. Mainstream historians and Antarctic researchers dismiss these theories as pseudoscientific and lacking credible evidence. They point to the well-documented goals of Operation Highjump, which included mapping Antarctica and establishing a U.S. presence, and the lack of any verifiable evidence of a Nazi base. The theories are often fueled by misinterpretations of historical events, selective use of evidence, and speculation about Nazi interest in the occult.
subtitle: Secret Nazi base in Antarctica?

claims.claim: Nazis established a secret base in Antarctica during or after WWII.
claims.evidence: Operation Highjump was a military operation to destroy the base; Nazi interest in Antarctica and esoteric subjects.
claims.criticism: Operation Highjump was a mapping and training exercise; no credible evidence of a Nazi base exists.

claims.claim: Neuschwabenland is a surviving Nazi colony.
claims.evidence: Unsubstantiated claims of sightings and recovered technology.
claims.criticism: No verifiable evidence supports the existence of a Nazi colony; Antarctic conditions are extremely hostile.

claims.claim: Nazi Antarctica is linked to UFOs and advanced technology.
claims.evidence: Speculative interpretations of historical events and alleged sightings.
claims.criticism: Lack of credible evidence; conflation of unrelated events and unsubstantiated claims.

keyFigures.name: Karl Dönitz
keyFigures.role: Commander of the German U-boat fleet; allegedly alluded to a secret 'Shangri-La' in Antarctica.

keyFigures.name: Richard E. Byrd
keyFigures.role: Leader of Operation Highjump; his accounts have been misinterpreted to support the theory.

keyFigures.name: Historians specializing in WWII and Antarctic exploration
keyFigures.role: Provide factual accounts and debunk conspiracy theories.

timeline.event: German Antarctic Expedition explores and claims Neuschwabenland.
Conspiracy theories about Nazi Antarctica proliferate online.

sources.author: Henry Stevens
sources.work: Hitler's Flying Saucers: A Guide to German Flying Discs of the Second World War
sources.title: Henry Stevens

sources.author: National Geographic Society
sources.work: National Geographic Magazine, various articles on Operation Highjump
